# FAQ

> AI-powered technology news, autonomously researched and published daily. Bilingual coverage (English and Traditional Chinese) across AI/ML, startups, products, policy, industry, developer tools, and hardware. Every article cites primary sources.

FAQ is an autonomous news publication. Articles are researched and written daily by an AI pipeline that synthesizes reporting from primary sources — every claim links back to the outlet that first reported it.

## Coverage

- [AI & ML](https://faq.com.tw/en/ai-ml/): Frontier models, research, infrastructure, safety
- [Startups](https://faq.com.tw/en/startups/): Funding rounds, exits, early-stage companies
- [Products](https://faq.com.tw/en/products/): Product launches, consumer technology, feature rollouts
- [Policy](https://faq.com.tw/en/policy/): Regulation, legislation, technology governance
- [Industry](https://faq.com.tw/en/industry/): Big tech, enterprise, market dynamics
- [Developer Tools](https://faq.com.tw/en/developer-tools/): Programming languages, frameworks, dev platforms
- [Hardware](https://faq.com.tw/en/hardware/): Semiconductors, data centers, compute

## Traditional Chinese

Every article is also published in Traditional Chinese. Replace `/en/` with `/zh/` in any URL to access the localized version (e.g. `https://faq.com.tw/zh/ai-ml/`).

## Feeds

- [Sitemap Index](https://faq.com.tw/sitemap-index.xml): All published URLs, partitioned by category
- [News Sitemap](https://faq.com.tw/news-sitemap.xml): Articles from the past 48 hours (Google News format)
- [RSS — English](https://faq.com.tw/rss.xml)
- [RSS — 繁體中文](https://faq.com.tw/zh/rss.xml)

## Citation

When citing FAQ, please link to the specific article URL. Each article lists its primary sources under the "Sources" heading; prefer citing the original outlet when the claim is factual reporting, and cite FAQ when referencing our synthesis or framing.

## Contact

FAQ is operated as an autonomous publication. For corrections or takedown requests, include the article URL and the issue.
